title: ललिता सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री ललिता सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् ॥

॥ विनियोगः ॥
अस्य श्री ललिता सहस्रनाम स्तोत्र महामन्त्रस्य
वशिन्यादिवाग्देवता ऋषयः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्री ललिता परमेश्वरी देवता।
ऐं बीजम्। ह्रीं शक्तिः। श्रीं कीलकम्।
श्री ललिता परमेश्वरी प्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
सिन्दूरारुणविग्रहां त्रिनयनां माणिक्यमौलिस्फुर-
त्तारानायकशेखरां स्मितमुखीमापीनवक्षोरुहाम्।
पाणिभ्यामलिपूर्णरत्नचषकं रक्तोत्पलं बिभ्रतीं
सौम्यां रत्नघटस्थरक्तचरणां ध्यायेत्परामम्बिकाम्॥१॥

ध्यायेत्पद्मासनस्थां विकसितवदनां पद्मपत्रायताक्षीं
हेमाभां पीतवस्त्रां करकलितलसद्धेमपद्मां वराङ्गीम्।
सर्वालंकारयुक्तां सततमभयदां भक्तनम्रां भवानीं
श्रीविद्यां शान्तमूर्तिं सकलसुरनुतां सर्वसम्पत्प्रदात्रीम्॥२॥

सकुंकुमविलेपनामलिकचुम्बिकस्तूरिकां
समन्दहसितेक्षणां सशरचापपाशांकुशाम्।
अशेषजनमोहिनीमरुणमाल्यभूषाम्बरां
जपाकुसुमभासुरां जपविधौ स्मरेदम्बिकाम्॥३॥

॥ स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ श्री माता श्री महाराज्ञी श्रीमत्सिंहासनेश्वरी।
चिदग्निकुण्डसम्भूता देवकार्यसमुद्यता॥१॥

उद्यद्भानुसहस्राभा चतुर्बाहुसमन्विता।
रागस्वरूपपाशाढ्या क्रोधाकाराङ्कुशोज्ज्वला॥२॥

मनोरूपेक्षुकोदण्डा पञ्चतन्मात्रसायका।
निजारुणप्रभापूरमज्जद्ब्रह्माण्डमण्डला॥३॥

चम्पकाशोकपुन्नागसौगन्धिकलसत्कचा।
कुरुविन्दमणिश्रेणीकनत्कोटीरमण्डिता॥४॥

अष्टमीचन्द्रविभ्राजदलिकस्थलशोभिता।
मुखचन्द्रकलंकाभमृगनाभिविशेषका॥५॥

वदनस्मरमांगल्यगृहतोरणचिल्लिका।
वक्त्रलक्ष्मीपरीवाहचलन्मीनाभलोचना॥६॥

नवचम्पकपुष्पाभनासादण्डविराजिता।
ताराकान्तितिरस्कारिनासाभरणभासुरा॥७॥

कदम्बमञ्जरीकॢप्तकर्णपूरमनोहरा।
ताटङ्कयुगलीभूततपनोडुपमण्डला॥८॥

पद्मरागशिलादर्शपरिभाविकपोलभूः।
नवविद्रुमबिम्बश्रीन्यक्कारिरदनच्छदा॥९॥

शुद्धविद्याङ्कुराकारद्विजपङ्क्तिद्वयोज्ज्वला।
कर्पूरवीटिकामोदसमाकर्षद्दिगन्तरा॥१०॥

निजसल्लापमाधुर्यविनिर्भर्त्सितकच्छपी।
मन्दस्मितप्रभापूरमज्जत्कामेशमानसा॥११॥

अनाकलितसादृश्यचिबुकश्रीविराजिता।
कामेशबद्धमांगल्यसूत्रशोभितकन्धरा॥१२॥

कनकाङ्गदकेयूरकमनीयभुजान्विता।
रत्नग्रैवेयचिन्ताकलोलमुक्ताफलान्विता॥१३॥

कामेश्वरप्रेमरत्नमणिप्रतिपणस्तनी।
नाभ्यालवालरोमालिलताफलकुचद्वयी॥१४॥

लक्ष्यरोमलताधारतासमुन्नेयमध्यमा।
स्तनभारदलन्मध्यपट्टबन्धवलित्रया॥१५॥

अरुणारुणकौसुम्भवस्त्रभास्वत्कटीतटी।
रत्नकिंकिणिकारम्यरशनादामभूषिता॥१६॥

कामेशज्ञातसौभाग्यमार्दवोरुद्वयान्विता।
माणिक्यमकुटाकारजानुद्वयविराजिता॥१७॥

इन्द्रगोपपरिक्षिप्तस्मरतूणाभजंघिका।
गूढगुल्फा कूर्मपृष्ठजयिष्णुप्रपदान्विता॥१८॥

नखदीधितिसंछन्ननमज्जनतमोगुणा।
पदद्वयप्रभाजालपराकृतसरोरुहा॥१९॥

शिञ्जानमणिमञ्जीरमण्डितश्रीपदाम्बुजा।
मरालीमन्दगमना महालावण्यशेवधिः॥२०॥

सर्वारुणा अनवद्यांगी सर्वाभरणभूषिता।
शिवकामेश्वराङ्कस्था शिवा स्वाधीनवल्लभा॥२१॥

सुमेरुमध्यशृंगस्था श्रीमन्नगरनायिका।
चिन्तामणिगृहान्तस्था पञ्चब्रह्मासनस्थिता॥२२॥

महापद्माटवीसंस्था कदम्बवनवासिनी।
सुधासागरमध्यस्था कामाक्षी कामदायिनी॥२३॥

देवर्षिगणसंघातस्तूयमानात्मवैभवा।
भण्डासुरवधोद्युक्तशक्तिसेनासमन्विता॥२४॥

सम्पत्करीसमारूढसिन्धुरव्रजसेविता।
अश्वारूढाधिष्ठिताश्वकोटिकोटिभिरावृता॥२५॥

॥ इति श्री ललिता सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् (प्रथम भागः — ध्यानम् एवं प्रथम ५०+ नामानि) ॥

॥ ॐ ललितायै नमः ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Lalita Sahasranama Stotram ||

|| Viniyogah ||
Asya Shri Lalita Sahasranama Stotra Mahamantrasya
Vashinyadi-Vag-devata Rishayah.
Anushtup Chhandah. Shri Lalita Parameshvari Devata.
Aim Bijam. Hrim Shaktih. Shrim Kilakam.
Shri Lalita Parameshvari Prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

|| Dhyanam ||
Sindura-aruna-vigraham Trinayanam Manikya-mauli-sphurat-
tara-nayaka-shekharam Smita-mukhim Apina-vakshorruham.
Panibhyam-alipurna-ratna-chashakam Raktotpalam Bibhratim
Saumyam Ratna-ghatasha-rakta-charanam Dhyayet Param-ambikam.||1||

Dhyayet Padmasana-stham Vikasita-vadanam Padma-patrayatakshim
Hemabham Pita-vastram Kara-kalita-lasad-dhema-padmam Varangim.
Sarvalamkara-yuktam Satatam-abhaya-dam Bhakta-namram Bhavanim
Shri-vidyam Shanta-murtim Sakala-sura-nutam Sarva-sampat-pradatrim.||2||

Sakunkuma-vilepanam-alika-chumbi-kasturikam
Samanda-hasitekshnam Sashara-chapa-pasha-ankusham.
Ashesha-jana-mohinim Aruna-malya-bhusha-ambaram
Japa-kusuma-bhasuram Japa-vidhau Smared-ambikam.||3||

|| Stotram ||

Om Shri Mata Shri Maharajni Shrimat-simhasana-eshvari.
Chid-agni-kunda-sambhuta Deva-karya-samudyata.||1||

Udyad-bhanu-sahasrabha Chatur-bahu-samanvita.
Raga-svarupa-pashadhya Krodha-akara-ankusho-jjvala.||2||

Mano-rupe-kshu-kodanda Pancha-tanmatra-sayaka.
Nijaruna-prabha-pura-majjad-brahmanda-mandala.||3||

Champaka-ashoka-punnaga-saugandhika-lasat-kacha.
Kuruvinda-mani-shreni-kanat-kotira-mandita.||4||

Ashtami-chandra-vibhrajad-alika-sthala-shobhita.
Mukha-chandra-kalanka-abha-mriganabhi-visheshaka.||5||

Vadana-smara-mangalya-griha-torana-chillika.
Vaktra-lakshmi-parivaha-chalan-minabha-lochana.||6||

Nava-champaka-pushpa-abha-nasa-danda-virajita.
Tara-kanti-tiraskari-nasa-bharana-bhasura.||7||

Kadamba-manjari-klripta-karna-pura-manohara.
Tatanka-yugali-bhuta-tapano-dupa-mandala.||8||

Padma-raga-shiladarsha-paribhavi-kapola-bhuh.
Nava-vidruma-bimba-shri-nyakkari-radana-chchhada.||9||

Shuddha-vidya-ankura-akara-dvija-pankti-dvayo-jjvala.
Karpura-vitika-amoda-samakarshad-digantara.||10||

Nija-sallapa-madhurya-vinirbhartsita-kachchhapi.
Manda-smita-prabha-pura-majjat-kamesha-manasa.||11||

Anakalita-sadrishya-chibuka-shri-virajita.
Kamesha-baddha-mangalya-sutra-shobhita-kandhara.||12||

Kanaka-angada-keyura-kamaniya-bhujanvita.
Ratna-graiveya-chintaka-lola-mukta-phalanvita.||13||

Kameshvara-prema-ratna-mani-pratipana-stani.
Nabhyalavala-romali-lata-phala-kucha-dvayi.||14||

Lakshya-roma-lata-dhara-ta-samunneya-madhyama.
Stana-bhara-dalan-madhya-patta-bandha-valitraya.||15||

Aruna-aruna-kausumbha-vastra-bhasvat-kati-tati.
Ratna-kinkini-karamya-rashana-dama-bhushita.||16||

Kamesha-jnata-saubhagya-mardavo-ru-dvayanvita.
Manikya-makuta-akara-janu-dvaya-virajita.||17||

Indragopa-parikshipta-smara-tunabha-janghika.
Gudha-gulpha Kurma-prishtha-jayishnu-prapadanvita.||18||

Nakha-didhiti-samchchhanna-namaj-jana-tamo-guna.
Pada-dvaya-prabha-jala-parakrita-saroruha.||19||

Shinjana-mani-manjira-mandita-shri-padambuja.
Marali-manda-gamana Maha-lavanya-shevadhih.||20||

Sarvaruna Anavadyangi Sarva-bharana-bhushita.
Shiva-kamesvara-ankastha Shiva Svadhina-vallabha.||21||

Sumeru-madhya-shringastha Shriman-nagara-nayika.
Chintamani-grihantastha Pancha-brahma-asana-sthita.||22||

Maha-padmatavi-samstha Kadamba-vana-vasini.
Sudha-sagara-madhyastha Kamakshi Kamadayini.||23||

Devarshi-gana-sanghata-stuyamana-atma-vaibhava.
Bhandasura-vadho-dyukta-shakti-sena-samanvita.||24||

Sampatkari-samarudha-sindhura-vraja-sevita.
Ashvarudha-adhishthita-ashva-koti-kotibhir-avrita.||25||

|| Iti Shri Lalita Sahasranama Stotram (Prathama Bhagah — Dhyanam evam Prathama 50+ Namani) ||

|| Om Lalitayai Namah ||
hindiMeaning:
श्री माता, श्री महाराज्ञी (महारानी), सिंहासन पर विराजमान, चित्-अग्नि-कुण्ड से प्रकट, देवताओं के कार्य के लिए उद्यत।

उदित होते सहस्र सूर्यों के समान कान्ति वाली, चार भुजाओं से युक्त। राग (प्रेम) स्वरूप पाश धारण करने वाली, क्रोध रूप अंकुश से उज्ज्वल।

मन रूपी इक्षु (गन्ने) का धनुष धारण करने वाली, पञ्च तन्मात्र (शब्द, स्पर्श, रूप, रस, गन्ध) रूपी पाँच बाण धारिणी। अपनी अरुण प्रभा की बाढ़ में समस्त ब्रह्माण्ड मण्डल को डुबोने वाली।

चम्पक, अशोक, पुन्नाग और सौगन्धिक पुष्पों से सुगन्धित केशों वाली। कुरुविन्द मणियों की श्रेणी से चमकते मुकुट से मण्डित।

अष्टमी के चन्द्रमा के समान चमकते ललाट वाली। मुख रूपी चन्द्रमा के कलंक (चिह्न) के समान कस्तूरी तिलक लगाने वाली।

ये सहस्रनाम ललिता परमेश्वरी (त्रिपुरसुन्दरी) के दिव्य गुणों, रूप, शक्तियों और महिमा का वर्णन करते हैं। इनके ऋषि वशिन्यादि वाग्देवताएँ हैं, छन्द अनुष्टुप् है और देवता ललिता परमेश्वरी हैं। यहाँ प्रथम 50+ नामों तक का पाठ दिया गया है।

viniyog.shloka: अस्य श्रीललितासहस्रनामस्तोत्रमहामन्त्रस्य वशिन्यादिवाग्देवता ऋषयः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीललिता महात्रिपुरसुन्दरी परमेश्वरी देवता। ऐं बीजम्। ह्रीं शक्तिः। श्रीं कीलकम्। श्रीललिता महात्रिपुरसुन्दरी प्रीत्यर्थे ललितासहस्रनामजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Supreme hymn containing 1000 names of Goddess Lalita Tripurasundari
Invokes all four purusharthas: dharma, artha, kama, and moksha
Invokes the blessing of beauty, grace, and magnetic personality on the reciter
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins, doshas, and karmic debts accumulated over lifetimes
Invokes deep wisdom, spiritual power, and kundalini awakening
Recitation fulfils all desires and leads to union with the Divine Mother
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Parvati, Lalita Sahasranama Stotram (ललिता सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्) stands as a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names. Rooted in Brahmanda Purana (Lalitopakhyana), this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to recitation fulfils all desires and leads to union with the Divine Mother as well as grant deep wisdom, spiritual power, and kundalini awakening. Additionally, it is known to grant all four purusharthas: dharma, artha, kama, and moksha. It is especially recommended on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri and Lalita Jayanti for maximum spiritual benefit. With 50 verses and a reading time of about 25 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Lalita Sahasranama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Lalita Sahasranama in Sanskrit with viniyog, dhyana shlokas, Hindi meaning. Sacred 1000 names of Goddess Lalita Tripurasundari.
relatedSearches: Lalita Sahasranama lyrics in Sanskrit Devanagari PDF

Lalita Sahasranama 1000 names list with meaning

seoFaqs.question: What is the Lalita Sahasranama and where is it preserved?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lalita Sahasranama is the thousand-name hymn of Goddess Lalita Tripurasundari, preserved in the Lalitopakhyana section of the Brahmanda Purana, specifically in the Uttara Khanda chapters 36 to 40. The text contains exactly 1000 divine names of the Goddess arranged in 182 to 183 anushtup verses (the count varies slightly across editions due to verse-division), preceded by a viniyoga, three dhyana shlokas and followed by an extensive phalashruti. It is regarded as the most authoritative thousand-name hymn of the Divine Mother in the Sri Vidya tradition and is recited daily in Sri Chakra worship and in temples of the Goddess across India, especially in Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh and Kerala. (Sources: Brahmanda Purana Uttara Khanda 36-40; Bhaskararaya Saubhagya-Bhaskara commentary; Hindupedia Lalita Sahasranama.)

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Lalita Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: The Lalita Sahasranama is described as not having a human author but as having been spoken by the Vag-devatas — the eight goddesses of speech named Vashini, Kameshi, Modini, Vimala, Aruna, Jayini, Sarveshvari and Kaulini — at the express command of Goddess Lalita herself in the assembly of Sri Nagara, after she had vanquished the demon Bhandasura. The viniyoga therefore names Vashinyadi Vagdevatah as the rishis of this mantra, anushtup as the metre, and Sri Lalita Parameshvari as the deity. The text is thus considered apaurusheya (not of human origin) within the Sri Vidya lineage, equal in authority to a Vedic revelation. (Sources: Lalitopakhyana 36; Bhaskararaya commentary; Sri Vidya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Goddess Lalita Tripurasundari?
seoFaqs.answer: Lalita Tripurasundari is the supreme form of the Divine Mother in the Sri Vidya tradition — also known as Rajarajeshwari, Kamakshi, Bala Tripurasundari, Maha Tripurasundari, Shodashi (in the Dasha Mahavidya), and the deity of the Sri Chakra yantra. She is described as sindura-aruna-vigraham — of the colour of the crimson dawn — three-eyed, four-armed, holding a noose (paasha) of love, a goad (ankusha) of restraint, a sugarcane bow representing the mind, and five flower arrows representing the five tan-matras (sound, touch, form, taste, smell). She presides from her capital city of Sri Nagara on Mount Mahameru with her consort Kameshvara (Shiva), surrounded by the Yoginis of the Sri Chakra. (Sources: Lalitopakhyana 8-15; Tripura Rahasya; Bhavanopanishad.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the connection between Lalita Sahasranama and Sri Vidya?
seoFaqs.answer: Lalita Sahasranama is the principal stotra of the Sri Vidya tradition — the most refined of the Shakta Tantric paths, focused on the worship of the Sri Chakra yantra and the panchadashi and shodashi mantras of Lalita Tripurasundari. The thousand names systematically map the Goddess onto the Sri Chakra: many names refer to the chakra's nine enclosures (avaranas), to its presiding Yoginis, to the kundalini chakras of the human body, and to the three esoteric kutas of the panchadashi mantra (vagbhava-kuta, kamaraja-kuta, shakti-kuta). Sri Vidya initiates use the Sahasranama as a daily archana with one name offered per kunkuma grain on the Sri Chakra. (Sources: Bhaskararaya Saubhagya-Bhaskara; Bhavanopanishad; Tripura Rahasya Jnana Khanda.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Lalita Sahasranama promise?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti at the end of the Lalita Sahasranama is exceptionally extensive. It promises freedom from disease, including chronic ailments and madness; protection from poison, weapons, fire, water and wild animals; victory over enemies in litigation and battle; relief from poverty; blessing of progeny for childless couples; auspiciousness for unmarried women seeking suitable husbands; longevity; eloquence; mastery over kingdoms; and ultimately, identity with the Goddess herself (sayujya). It particularly emphasises that recitation by women on Fridays brings saubhagya (marital good fortune), and that recitation in Sri Chakra puja with kunkuma archana confers all of the Sri Vidya siddhis. (Sources: Lalita Sahasranama phalashruti; Bhaskararaya commentary on phalashruti.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the most auspicious time to recite Lalita Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: Friday (Shukravar) is the day specifically sacred to Lalita and the most universally observed time for recitation. Special occasions include Lalita Jayanti (Magha Shukla Purnima, the appearance day of the Goddess), Lalita Panchami (Ashvin Shukla Panchami, falling within Sharad Navratri), all nine days of both Sharad and Vasanta Navratri, Tuesday (the day of Tripurasundari for some lineages), every full moon day, the eight Ashtami tithis of Goddess worship, and the three sandhya periods of dawn, noon and dusk. In Sri Vidya households the recitation is performed before the Sri Chakra with kunkuma archana, ideally between 4 and 6 AM during Brahma muhurta. (Sources: drikpanchang.com Lalita Jayanti; Sri Vidya nitya-puja paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Lalita Sahasranama, Lalita Trishati and Bala Sahasranama?
seoFaqs.answer: All three are Sri Vidya hymns to forms of the Divine Mother but differ in scope and use. The Lalita Sahasranama (1000 names) is the principal text from the Brahmanda Purana, used for the most extensive daily and festival worship. The Lalita Trishati (300 names) is a shorter Sri Vidya text, also from the Brahmanda Purana, where each of the fifteen letters of the panchadashi mantra is glorified through twenty names, making it the most direct mantra-vyakhyana. The Bala Sahasranama is the thousand-name hymn of Bala Tripurasundari, the youthful nine-year-old form of the Goddess, used by initiates in Bala-vidya practice. Most practitioners progress from Bala-mantra and Bala Sahasranama to the Panchadashi and Lalita Sahasranama as they advance in Sri Vidya. (Sources: Brahmanda Purana; Sri Vidya Sampradaya literature.)

seoFaqs.question: Who was Bhandasura and why is he mentioned in the dhyana?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhandasura was a powerful demon born from the ashes of Kamadeva (the god of love) after Shiva had burned him with the third eye. He grew to dominate the three worlds and oppressed both gods and sages. The Lalitopakhyana narrates that the assembled devas performed a great yajna in which they offered their own flesh into the fire, from which Lalita Tripurasundari emerged seated on the Chakra-ratha, an enormous nine-storeyed war chariot embodying the Sri Chakra. With her army of Shaktis (Sampatkari on the elephant Vighnesha and Ashvarudha on the horse-mount Aparajita, both named in verses 24-25 of the Sahasranama), she defeated Bhandasura. The story is the mythological backdrop that explains the entire structure of the Sri Chakra and the Sahasranama. (Sources: Brahmanda Purana Lalitopakhyana 14-30; Bhaskararaya commentary.)

seoFaqs.question: How long does it take to recite Lalita Sahasranama and how should it be performed?
seoFaqs.answer: A complete recitation including viniyoga, three dhyana shlokas, the 1000 names in 182 anushtup verses, and the phalashruti takes approximately 35 to 45 minutes at moderate pace, or up to one hour with kunkuma archana on the Sri Chakra (offering one pinch of kunkuma at each name). Traditional vidhi: bathe, wear clean clothes (red or pink for Lalita), perform achamana, light a lamp with sesame oil or ghee before the Sri Chakra or Lalita murti, offer red flowers (especially hibiscus), red kunkuma, sandal paste and naivedya. Recite the viniyoga, the three dhyanas (Sindhuraruna-vigraham, Dhyayet-padmasanasthitam, Sakunkuma-vilepana), then the names with full attention, then the phalashruti, then the arati. (Sources: Sri Vidya nitya-puja paddhati; Devi Bhagavatam ritual sections.)
